tasks.title: Shared aria-live announcer (useAnnouncer store + <Announcer/>)
tasks.description: Add a tiny Zustand store `useAnnouncer` ({ message, announce(msg) }) in frontend/src/stores/useAnnouncer.ts and a visually-hidden `role="status" aria-live="polite"` component `<Announcer/>` in frontend/src/components/shared/Announcer.tsx that renders `message`. Force re-announcement of identical strings via a nonce/counter so the text node changes. Mount <Announcer/> once (in Board). SR-only styling (sr-only), no visual footprint.
acceptance_criteria: useAnnouncer exposes `message: string` and `announce(msg: string)`.
Calling announce twice with the same string still updates the rendered text node (nonce/counter).
<Announcer/> renders one element with role="status" and aria-live="polite", visually hidden (sr-only) but present in the accessibility tree.
No visible UI change on the board.

tasks.title: Board keyboard-reorder handler (reuse computeDropRank + reorderTask)
tasks.description: Add `handleKeyboardReorder(taskId, column, direction: 'up'|'down')` to Board.tsx. Resolve the card's neighbor from the RENDERED/visible order for that column (respect arc filter and arc grouping; see T-005), compute the target rank by reusing the existing computeDropRank (overTaskId = neighbor, insertBefore = true for 'up' / false for 'down'), then call reorderTask (or loop the rebalance branch when needsRebalance). After a successful move, call announce("Task '<title>' moved to position N of M in <Column label>"). Guard: ignore when isMutating. Pass the handler down through Column to TaskCard/CardActionMenu.
acceptance_criteria: Handler reuses the existing computeDropRank; no duplicated rank math.
'up' moves the card one slot toward index 0; 'down' one slot toward the end, matching drag semantics.
needsRebalance path loops reorderTask over rebalancedTasks exactly as the drop handler does.
After each successful move announce() is called with the correct 1-based position and column label.
No store call is made while isMutating is true.
Handler prop is threaded Board -> Column -> TaskCard/CardActionMenu.

tasks.title: TaskCard focusability + Alt+Arrow keydown + focus-visible ring
tasks.description: Make the TaskCard <article> focusable (tabIndex={0}), add aria-keyshortcuts="Alt+ArrowUp Alt+ArrowDown", and an onKeyDown that, only when e.altKey && key is ArrowUp/ArrowDown, calls e.preventDefault()+e.stopPropagation() and invokes onKeyboardReorder(task.id, column, dir). All other keys (Tab, Enter/Space to open panel, Escape) must pass through unaffected. Add a visible focus-visible ring (focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-primary focus-visible:outline-hidden) — none exists today. Use design-system tokens only (no inline styles).
acceptance_criteria: Card is reachable by Tab and shows a visible focus ring (2.4.7).
Alt+ArrowUp / Alt+ArrowDown on a focused card trigger a reorder and are the only keys intercepted.
Tab, Enter/Space (open panel), and Escape behavior are unchanged.
aria-keyshortcuts is present on the card.
Only Tailwind design-system tokens used; no style={{}} added.

tasks.title: CardActionMenu move-up / move-down buttons (discoverable + WCAG 2.5.7)
tasks.description: Add onMoveUp?/onMoveDown? props to CardActionMenu and render two icon buttons (arrow_upward / arrow_downward) with aria-label "Move up"/"Move down" and a title tooltip that includes the shortcut hint (e.g. "Move up (Alt+↑)"). Wire them to Board's handleKeyboardReorder via TaskCard. Disable each at the list boundary (first card can't move up, last can't move down) and while isMutating. Follow the existing 28x28 icon-button styling. Final visual grouping relative to move-left/right is per the UX stage's wireframes.
acceptance_criteria: Move-up/down buttons appear in the card action overlay, keyboard-focusable and operable.
Buttons are disabled (and announced disabled) at column boundaries and while isMutating.
Clicking a button performs the same reorder as the Alt+Arrow shortcut (single shared handler).
aria-labels present; tooltip includes the shortcut hint.
Provides the non-drag pointer alternative required by WCAG 2.5.7.

tasks.title: Arc-group / arc-filter boundary handling in reorder
tasks.description: Ensure neighbor resolution uses the visible ordering. When arc grouping is ON, constrain reorder to within the card's arc group: a press that would cross into another group or the ungrouped bucket is a no-op that announces the boundary (moving across groups would implicitly change the card's arc). When an arc filter is active, compute the neighbor from the filtered visible list. Boundary presses (top ^ / bottom v) never call reorderTask and announce 'already at top/bottom of <Column>'.
acceptance_criteria: With grouping on, a card at the top of its group does not move into the previous group; a spoken boundary announcement is made.
With an arc filter active, reorder places the card relative to visible neighbors only.
First/last visible card boundary presses make no store call and announce the boundary.
No press ever changes a card's arc.

tasks.title: Tests — unit, component (RTL), and keyboard focus retention
tasks.description: Unit tests for neighbor/rank resolution incl. boundaries, arc-group constraint, and rebalance path. Component (RTL) tests: Alt+ArrowUp/Down calls reorderTask with the expected rank; plain arrows/Tab/Enter are NOT swallowed; announcer text is correct; boundary press announces and does not call reorderTask; buttons disabled at boundaries. Assert focus stays on the moved card after reorder. Target >90% coverage on new/changed lines.
acceptance_criteria: Unit tests cover up/down, both boundaries, arc-group constraint, and rebalance.
Component tests assert reorderTask call args, announcement strings, and that non-shortcut keys pass through.
A test asserts the moved card retains focus after reorder.
>90% coverage on new/changed frontend lines; frontend `npm test` green.

tasks.title: E2E keyboard reorder + persistence (Playwright) and changelog
tasks.description: Playwright keyboard-only E2E: Tab to a card, Alt+ArrowDown, assert DOM order changed and focus remains on the same card; reload and assert order persisted. Verify the aria-live region and focus-visible ring exist. Add a changelog entry describing the accessible reorder path and shortcuts.
acceptance_criteria: Headless Playwright test reorders a card with the keyboard and verifies persisted order after reload.
Test verifies focus retention and presence of the live region.
Changelog updated with the feature and its keyboard shortcuts.
